question_text: Which of the following best describes the difference between a series circuit and a parallel circuit?

options.label: A
options.text: In series, current flows in one path; in parallel, current has multiple paths to take

options.label: B
options.text: Series circuits use batteries and parallel circuits use generators

options.label: C
options.text: Parallel circuits have higher voltage than series circuits

options.label: D
options.text: Series circuits are brighter than parallel circuits

explanation: The key difference is the path of current. In a series circuit, there is only one loop for electricity to follow. In a parallel circuit, the current can split and take multiple different paths through different branches, each containing components.
